gleam 0.0.0.9000
  • Reference

Calculate Soil Organic Carbon Stock Change

Source: R/soil_carbon_core_model.R
calc_soil_carbon.Rd

Computes the change in soil organic carbon (SOC) stock between two management states over a 20-year period. This is a Tier 1 stock-change equation with a fixed 20-year transition period.

calc_soil_carbon(
  area,
  climate_zone,
  soil_carbon_reference = NA_real_,
  soil_type,
  management_start,
  management_end,
  management_params,
  soil_type_params,
  luc_factors
)

Arguments

area

Numeric. Land area (ha).

climate_zone

Character. IPCC climate zone code (e.g., "TropicalMoist", "WarmTemperateDry").

soil_carbon_reference

Numeric. Reference SOC value (t C/ha, optional). If missing, will be looked up from soil_type_params.

soil_type

Character. IPCC soil type code (e.g., "HighActivityClay", "LowActivityClay", "Sandy", "Spodic"). Required if soil_carbon_reference is missing.

management_start

Character. Starting management type (e.g., "ImprovedMediumInput", "SeverelyDegraded").

management_end

Character. Ending management type (e.g., "NonDegraded", "HighIntensityGrazing").

management_params

Data.table. Stock change factors for management regime (dimensionless). Must have columns: climate_zone, management_type, V1.

soil_type_params

Data.table. Reference SOC values by climate zone and soil type (t C/ha). Must have columns: climate_zone, soil_type, V1.

luc_factors

Data.table. Stock change factors for land use or land-use change type (dimensionless). Must have columns: climate_zone, V1.

Value

List. Contains:

SOC1

Soil organic carbon stock at the beginning of the period (t C total).

SOC2

Soil organic carbon stock at the end of the period (t C total).

dSOC

Annual change in soil carbon stock over 20 years (t C/year total).

Details

The core calculation is: $$SOC1 = area \times socRef \times mgmtFactor_{start} \times lucFactor$$ $$SOC2 = area \times socRef \times mgmtFactor_{end} \times lucFactor$$ $$dSOC = (SOC2 - SOC1) / 20$$

Contents

Developed by Ahmed Jou, Yassine Elaouni, Dominik Wisser, Lydia Lanzoni, Giuseppe Tempio, Federal Ministry of Agriculture, Food and Regional Identity of Germany BMELH.

Site built with pkgdown 2.1.3.
